title: COPY --exclude: make patterns context relative
body:
Prepend each copy source to each exclude so source relative patterns match.

> Note: I found that the conformance tests ignore cases where the Buildah image contains more items than the Docker image. This is problematic for exclusion.

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/6726

comments.body: Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
@nalind, I think PR is ready for review.

I remember there was also an issue that `podman` didn't set new permissions when overwriting subdirs in consequent COPY. That's why I had to use `--exclude` (which didn't help).

Now I have this in Docker. to set write access. without `--exclude`. I haven't tested that it works the same way with `podman`.
```
COPY app/ app/
RUN ls -la app/storage
# copy write enabled dirs separately to set correct permissions
COPY --parents --chown=ubuntu:ubuntu \
     app/storage \
     ./
```

> I remember there was also an issue that `podman` didn't set new permissions when overwriting subdirs in consequent COPY. That's why I had to use `--exclude` (which didn't help).
> 
> Now I have this in Docker. to set write access. without `--exclude`. I haven't tested that it works the same way with `podman`.
> 
> ```
> COPY app/ app/
> RUN ls -la app/storage
> # copy write enabled dirs separately to set correct permissions
> COPY --parents --chown=ubuntu:ubuntu \
>      app/storage \
>      ./
> ```

I think this is unrelated to this PR.
@nalind PTAL, I have addressed your comments.
